title: Security Not Built Into the Architecture
summary: Authentication, authorization, trust boundaries, secret handling, and data protection are treated as afterthoughts.
inspection_goal: Inspect whether trust boundaries and security-sensitive paths are explicit and structurally respected.
primary_signals: Sensitive stores directly reachable from many components
Cross-boundary calls without clear trust mediation
Security-sensitive responsibilities embedded in unrelated modules
Secret or identity handling concentrated in ad hoc locations
inspection_questions: Where are the trust boundaries in this graph?
Which components touch sensitive data or identity flows?
Are access paths narrower than data sensitivity would require?
Does security logic appear centralized and explicit or scattered and implicit?
evidence_to_collect: Breadth of access to databases or external systems
Context clues around auth, secret, and policy handling
Boundary analysis around externally exposed nodes
recommendation_patterns: Centralize security-sensitive capabilities behind stable interfaces
Reduce broad access to sensitive stores and operations
Separate security policy enforcement from unrelated application logic

severity_guidance.high: Security-sensitive paths are broad, implicit, and structurally hard to contain.
severity_guidance.medium: Important controls exist but boundaries are inconsistent.
severity_guidance.low: Security responsibilities appear explicit and structurally bounded.
